title: first-class tape values
manager: Tapes become first-class values you can hold, pass, serialize, and inspect — not just an internal recording mechanism but data a machine or its host can manipulate. This is what lets you save a run's tape, ship it in a bug report, diff two runs, or feed a recorded input tape back in to re-drive a machine, turning replay from a built-in feature into a composable capability you build tooling on.
cs: first-class tape values; construct/serialize/inspect; feed recorded input tapes back to re-drive a machine; the value-level counterpart to the tape wiring (W7.9).
